paper_title: Isotopic Analysis of T. occidentalis Leaf Samples

methods.description: Process for collecting and preparing T. occidentalis leaf samples for isotopic analysis, including cleaning, drying, and homogenizing the samples.

steps.description: Collect leaf material of T. occidentalis specimens from herbaria and natural present-day occurrences across a range of climate conditions.
steps.searchable_summary: collect T. occidentalis leaf samples from herbaria and natural sites

steps.description: Clean branches in an ultrasonic bath of deionized water to remove surface debris, oven dry at 50 C for 48 h, and homogenize.
steps.searchable_summary: clean, dry, and homogenize leaf samples

methods.description: Measurement of %C, %N, and d 13 C values in T. occidentalis specimens using elemental analyzer and CRDS.

steps.description: Place aliquots of each T. occidentalis specimen into tin capsules and measure %C and %N using a Costech elemental analyzer.
Measure d 13 C values using a Picarro G2201-i cavity ring-down spectrometer (CRDS).
steps.searchable_summary: measure d 13 C values using CRDS

steps.description: Run duplicates on both machines to ensure homogeneity.

methods.description: Calculation of carbon isotope fractionation values (D leaf) to decouple human-driven effects on d 13 C leaf values.

steps.description: Calculate D leaf values using known yearly d 13 C atm values.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate D leaf using d 13 C atm values

methods.description: Derivation of environmental data affecting d 13 C leaf values using global databases.

steps.description: Derive environmental data (MAP, MAT, and maximum summer temperature) from global databases using exact latitude and longitude coordinates.

methods.description: Statistical analysis of isotope values against climate variables to identify potential drivers.

steps.description: Calculate five-point moving averages of d 13 C leaf values to eliminate random noise.
steps.searchable_summary: compute moving averages of d 13 C leaf values

steps.description: Regress isotope values against climate variables using linear least squares regression.
steps.searchable_summary: perform linear regression on isotope and climate data

steps.description: Calculate p-values using the F-test to determine the chance of null hypothesis.

methods.description: Creation of a map showing the sampling locations using R and associated packages.

steps.description: Create a map of sampling locations using R version 3.5.0 and the ggplot2 and maps packages.
steps.searchable_summary: generate map of sampling locations using R
